prompt: ELI5: why are ARO variables immutable?
actual: ARO variables are immutable because they are designed to be simple and safe. Once a variable is assigned a value, it cannot be changed. This makes code easier to understand and less error-prone because you don't have to worry about variables changing unexpectedly.
expected: Each name binds to one value forever. To change a value you make a new binding with a new name (`<updated-count>`). That way you can never accidentally read the old value somewhere else — and the runtime can safely run independent statements in parallel.
